description:
You have assumed the role of a platform-engineer who has just received a
    request from the SRE team:  

    “Please give us a quick, reproducible view of how much disk space the
    existing Kubernetes YAML manifests actually occupy on disk.  
    Put the result in a single log file that we can ship to our long-term
    monitoring bucket.”

    A small collection of manifests is already checked-in under  
    /home/user/k8s/manifests/  
    (you do **not** need to create or change those YAML files; they are already
    present).

    Your task is to build an on-demand **disk-usage report** that satisfies all
    of the following functional requirements:

    1. Location & name  
       • The report **must** be written to the **exact** path  
         /home/user/k8s-reports/manifest_disk_usage.log  
       • Create the /home/user/k8s-reports/ directory yourself if it does not
         exist.

    2. Required output format (the automated test will parse this verbatim)  
       • Plain ASCII text, no colouring, no extra whitespace.  
       • One line per manifest, followed by one summary line.  
       • Each manifest line **must** be:  
             <absolute-path>|<size-in-bytes>  
         where  
         – <absolute-path> is the full path to the file  
         – <size-in-bytes> is the exact file size as an **integer** obtained
           from the filesystem (not rounded, no units, no commas).  
       • Lines **must** be sorted in **descending** order by
         <size-in-bytes>.  
       • After the last manifest line, append exactly one summary line:  
             TOTAL_BYTES=<sum-of-all-bytes>  
         where the sum is the **exact arithmetic sum** of the individual
         byte-counts already shown above, again with no commas or units.  
       • There must be **no blank lines** anywhere in the file.

       Concrete, correct example (values here are illustrative only – you have
       to extract the real ones from the live files):

           /home/user/k8s/manifests/deploy_backend.yaml|2310
           /home/user/k8s/manifests/deploy_frontend.yaml|1560
           /home/user/k8s/manifests/service_backend.yaml|830
           /home/user/k8s/manifests/service_frontend.yaml|780
           /home/user/k8s/manifests/configmap_env.yaml|500
           TOTAL_BYTES=5980

       The automated checker will look for the **exact same lines** (with the
       real byte counts present on disk) in exactly that order.

    3. Constraints  
       • Use only standard GNU/Linux user-land tooling available in a minimal
         distribution (e.g. awk, sort, stat, du, cat, printf, etc.).  
       • Do **not** require root privileges.  
       • The workflow should be entirely reproducible from a non-interactive
         shell script (so avoid commands that open editors or use interactive
         prompts).  

    4. What the SRE team expects to see after you are finished  
       • The path /home/user/k8s-reports/manifest_disk_usage.log exists.  
       • The file’s contents strictly match the output format described above.  

    5. Verification hints (for you, the engineer – optional)  
       • Run stat --format=%s <file> to obtain byte-accurate file sizes.  
       • Use printf or echo –n to ensure no trailing spaces.  
       • A quick self-check: `wc -l` on the log should give “number_of_manifests
         + 1” lines.

    No further deliverables are required.  Once the report is in place and
    correctly formatted, the task is complete.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data for automated evaluation.
        The manifests are pre-seeded in the image with the following exact sizes.
        The content itself is irrelevant to the agent, but the byte counts must
        stay immutable so that the checker can validate the report.

        Pre-existing directory tree:

        /home/user/k8s/manifests/
        ├── configmap_env.yaml        (500  bytes)
        ├── deploy_backend.yaml       (2310 bytes)
        ├── deploy_frontend.yaml      (1560 bytes)
        ├── service_backend.yaml      (830  bytes)
        └── service_frontend.yaml     (780  bytes)

        Expected full and precise contents of the report file that the agent
        must create:

        /home/user/k8s-reports/manifest_disk_usage.log  (ASCII, newline-LF)

        Line-ordered content:
            /home/user/k8s/manifests/deploy_backend.yaml|2310\n
            /home/user/k8s/manifests/deploy_frontend.yaml|1560\n
            /home/user/k8s/manifests/service_backend.yaml|830\n
            /home/user/k8s/manifests/service_frontend.yaml|780\n
            /home/user/k8s/manifests/configmap_env.yaml|500\n
            TOTAL_BYTES=5980\n
        (No additional whitespace before or after each newline.)

        During evaluation the harness will:
            1. Confirm that /home/user/k8s-reports/manifest_disk_usage.log exists.
            2. Read its entire contents and compare to the exact 6-line string
               above (byte-for-byte match).
    -->
name: task_000000_7f3c2fe3
